purpose: Durable lessons, failed assumptions, and reusable trial-and-error memory.

lesson_entry.category: false_positive | false_negative | flaky_behavior | fallback_misuse | boundary_confusion | reusable_heuristic

promote_when: a false positive or false negative pattern repeats
a release versus modeled boundary is confused
an E2E flaky behavior reveals a durable lesson
read_priority: lessons tagged both active-implementation and historical-cutover
do_not_store: transient step-by-step working memory already covered by work-rag.json

promotion_checklist: Is the lesson reusable beyond the current step?
Does it have at least one evidence reference?
Would a future agent or skill benefit from it?
Is it different from transient work state already tracked in work-rag.json?

example_lesson_entry.summary: Implemented modeled stages were mistaken for released workflow scope.
example_lesson_entry.lesson: Always read the released ceiling from the benchmark PRD first, even if higher modeled contracts already exist in code or docs.
example_lesson_entry.trigger: A later workflow task was considered in-bounds because Phase C-F contracts already existed.

lessons.lesson: ez-ax is orchestration-only; OpenClaw is the sole browser-facing actor. Once Python-first bootstrap is active, all legacy TypeScript/JS assets are invalid. When hardening an already-released scaffold, do not widen scope beyond approved ceiling.

The released ceiling is runCompletion with all 12 missions released (attach through runCompletion). The prior ceiling pageReadyObserved was expanded on 2026-03-26. When a scope ceiling string is unrecognized, default to the tightest boundary. Log typed failure artifacts when a run claims the release ceiling. Cross-check PRD assertions against the coverage ledger before promoting scope.

Validate mission identifiers, predecessor missions, duplicate-mission rejection, stop_reason/failure_detail mutual exclusion, and OpenClaw request/result identity at every typed boundary. Enforce comparability by rejecting duplicate target missions in checkpoint collections.

Low-attention continuation depends on phase/milestone/anchor coordinates in work-rag.json. When layered entrypoints exist, every shared autonomous skill must quote the exact on-disk next_action verbatim. Under a fixed ceiling and one-commit-safe rule, the autoloop prompt must instruct reading core-loop.md first.

Released checkpoint guards should enforce attach-first continuation, not gate initial creation. When autonomous continuation depends on work-rag.json, require the model to quote the exact on-disk value before acting, preventing stale-state false positives.

FINAL_STOP is a hard halt. When work-rag.current.next_action starts with 'FINAL_STOP — ', the autoloop runner returns 0 without invoking the LLM; the prior 'resume-search then seed one new slice' pattern was the root cause of the R32-R89 coverage-theater loop (see rag-archive.json archived 2026-04-23 and docs/prd-direction-realignment.md §2.3). Do not re-introduce auto-seeding past FINAL_STOP: resuming requires a new PRD-backed queue item.

Treat dependency/toolchain warnings (e.g., broken venv symlinks to macOS paths) as environment signals, not code bugs. The .venv/bin/python symlink targets a macOS homebrew path that is invalid inside the Linux sandbox.
